title: Checkpoints versus savepoints
description: How automatic checkpoints enable failure recovery while manual savepoints enable planned upgrades and rescaling.
body: Use when planning recovery and upgrades for a stateful job. Checkpoints are periodic, engine-managed snapshots used to recover automatically after a failure; savepoints are manually triggered, durable snapshots used to stop, upgrade code or rescale, and resume from a known state. Do not redeploy a stateful job without a savepoint — restarting from scratch loses accumulated state. Actions: rely on checkpoints for fault recovery and take a savepoint before any code or parallelism change. Validation signals include a restart resuming from a checkpoint and an upgrade resuming from a savepoint with intact state. Risk boundary: an incompatible state migration breaks resume; if state schema changed, escalate to a migration plan before restoring.
